content.problemStatement:
Chef has bought a new robot, which will be used for delivering dishes to his customers. He started testing the robot by letting it move on a line.

Initially, the robot is placed at the coordinate x=Xx = Xx=X. Then, it should execute a sequence of NNN commands, described by a string SSS with length NNN. Each character of this string is either 'L' or 'R', denoting that the robot should walk one step to the left (decreasing xxx by 111) or to the right (increasing xxx by 111), respectively.

How many distinct points are visited by the robot when it has executed all the commands? A point ppp is visited by the robot if ppp is an integer and the robot's position before or after executing some command is x=px = px=p.
constraints: S consists only of characters 'L' and 'R'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X — the length of the command string and the initial coordinate of the robot. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of characters 'L' and 'R'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer — the number of distinct integer points visited by the robot (including the starting point).

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test: positions visited are 10,11,12,11,10,9,8 → distinct {8,9,10,11,12} = 5. Second test: positions 0,-1,-2 → 3 distinct.

solution.approach: We simulate the robot's movement. Starting from X we keep a variable cur for the current position and insert it into a set. For each character in S we update cur by -1 for 'L' or +1 for 'R' and insert the new position into the set. The size of the set after processing the whole string is exactly the number of distinct points visited. This works because a set stores each distinct integer only once. The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case with O(N) additional memory for the set.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(N_total) across all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the set of visited positions.
